title: [security] Schnorr48 verification uses vartime operations risking timing attacks
description: crypto_eddsa_recover_r in lichen/subsys/lichen/crypto/monocypher.c:3001 uses ge_frombytes_neg_vartime(&minus_A, public_key) and slide_step with data-dependent branches/loops (lines 3028-3034). While public-key verification often tolerates this, combined with LICHEN truncated 48-byte Schnorr (spec/drafts/draft-lichen-schnorr-00.md) and LoRa link layer (MIC verification), it may leak via cache/timing on embedded platforms (nRF52840, ESP32). Monocypher main Ed25519 verify also uses vartime. Recommend constant-time alternatives or constant-time blinding if used in sensitive contexts. Check against test vectors in test/vectors/schnorr48.json.
